response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r1c0 is in critical survival mode: islanded with only 0.20 kWh (10% SoC) against a 1.00 kW load, yielding ~12 minutes of autonomy. All recent outbound sharing requests have been rejected by peers (r0c0, r1c1, r2c0) citing cluster-wide freefall, distrusted peer status, and insufficient SoC thresholds. The cluster appears to be in cascading reserve depletion with r0c0 at 11% and a 22-minute collective depletion window. r1c0 is explicitly listed as distrusted by r2c0, and all peers have shifted to inbound-only mode pending grid restoration or aggregator emergency injection. The fallback geographic round-robin policy has proven ineffective; immediate priority must shift to (1) aggressive inbound requests with urgent escalation, (2) strict self-preservation to avoid sub-5% SoC collapse, and (3) signaling to aggregator for emergency supply injection as the only viable pathway.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.75
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r2c0
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster in critical freefall; r1c0 distrusted by r2c0; all peers inbound-only. SoC=10% with 12-min autonomy. Outbound sharing suspended. Aggregator emergency injection mandatory. Inbound requests only with urgency=urgent."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
